Phase-33.NAME: MTProto Admin Ops and Observability
Phase-33.Goal: Add operator controls and redacted observability for MTProto assignment lifecycle and runtime bridge health.

NonGoals.item-2: No shared 443 cutover in this phase.

Steps.step-1: Add admin API for redacted assignment list, user assignment detail, runtime health, reissue, and revoke.
Steps.step-2: Add audit events for reissue and revoke without storing full links, verification tokens, or raw secrets.
Steps.step-3: Add admin frontend surface with mobile-safe search, status filters, bridge health, and confirmation-safe actions.
Steps.step-4: Add log markers for issue, replay, degraded runtime, reissue, and revoke paths.
Steps.step-5: Add backend/admin frontend verification for redaction, role gates, and destructive confirmation flows.

Verification.command: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py tests/test_admin_device_control.py -v

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Admins can find assignment status and bridge health without seeing raw secrets by default.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Reissue and revoke are explicit audited actions.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Revoking MTProto does not disable KrotPN subscription or VPN devices.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Admin mobile console remains compact and confirmation-safe.

Risks.risk-1: Admin screens can accidentally expose user proxy secrets if response schemas are too broad.
Risks.risk-2: Reissue behavior after secret rotation must be clear or users will hold stale Telegram links.
